channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
item.description: Discover how astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein’s theory of general relativity.

item.title: Deploying Nextcloud Private Cloud Storage on Ubuntu 24.04
item.description: Nextcloud is an open-source collaboration platform that provides file sync and share, calendars, contacts, video calls, and office collaboration on infrastructure you control. This guide deploys...

item.title: Deploying ownCloud File Sharing Platform on Ubuntu 24.04
item.description: ownCloud is an open-source file sharing platform that lets you store, sync, and share files from a self-hosted server, with desktop, mobile, and web clients across every major platform. This guide...

item.title: US President says 'I love the inflation'
item.description: Article URL: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/10/trump-inflation-cpi-iran-oil.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48483445 Points: 6 # Comments: 0

item.title: Cats Disappear at Sunset. Can You Find Them in Time?
item.description: This is a submission for the June Solstice Game Jam What I Built For this game jam, I built Find Them Before They Disappear, a hidden-object browser game where you find target cats before sunset...

item.title: I'm so tired to code. Not even Vibe Coding... D:
Nearly a million passports and photo IDs were left unprotected on the public internet
item.description: Typing a few letters and numbers into my web browser, I find myself gaping at the identity documents of complete strangers. The passport of a young woman from Germany. The passport of a man from...

item.title: I Built a Multi-Platform Publishing CLI for AI Agents
item.description: I Built a Multi-Platform Publishing CLI for AI Agents I contribute to open-source repos regularly. Every time, I'd manually copy-paste the same article to Dev.to, GitHub, LinkedIn — each with...

item.title: I built an embedded scheduler in Rust because I was tired of adding Redis just to run a background job
item.description: Every time I needed to run a background job in a Python app, the answer was always the same: add Redis, add Celery, add a worker process, add a broker. Just to send an email every morning. So I...

item.title: AWS Graviton: Cómo mejorar el Precio-Rendimiento de tus workloads en AWS
item.description: Introducción Reducir costos en la nube suele requerir iniciativas complejas: optimización de recursos, rediseño de arquitecturas, revisión de licencias o implementación de prácticas FinOps. Sin...

item.title: From Keypoints to Measurements: Why Landmarks Alone Are Useless
item.description: Every hand-tracking demo shows you 21 dots. The interesting part is what nobody shows: turning dots into numbers someone can act on. Dots are a capability, not a product Run any modern...

item.title: I Put a Neural Network Inside My Portfolio — No TensorFlow, No Server, 145 KB
item.description: Training a network from scratch in raw NumPy, quantizing it to int8, and running it as ~80 lines of dependency-free JavaScript — with a parity test proving the browser matches Python to 1e-6. Why...

item.title: Xbox warns of a ‘reset’ as it prepares for layoffs
item.description: Microsoft's Xbox division will be hit with significant layoffs next month, according to people familiar with Microsoft's plans. The company has been preparing for the layoffs internally for weeks,...

item.title: Apple, Google add support for Thread 1.4
item.description: Apple and Google are updating their smart home streaming devices to Thread 1.4. As first spotted by Matter Alpha and 9to5 Google, the latest spec has arrived on compatible Apple TVs in the tvOS 27...

item.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
item.description: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

item.title: Kalshi adds required employment verification for some prediction market bets
item.description: The CFTC is considering its first regulation for prediction markets, as arrests over "insider trading" on everything from military operations to Google Search data continue to stack up. As...

item.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
item.description: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic...

item.title: Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
item.description: New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out. In a blog post running more than 3,100...

item.title: The future of AI regulation is courting the strangest, most anxious bedfellows
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about tech politics, tech influence, and tech shenanigans in Washington, DC. (If you're not a subscriber, you can get on board...

item.title: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
item.description: If you've uploaded a song to YouTube, Google almost certainly considers your video fair game for training its Lyria music AI, it just won't admit it right now. A group of independent musicians is...

item.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
item.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

item.title: You can just tell the Instagram algorithm what you want now
item.description: Instagram is going to let you tweak what its algorithm shows you on your main feed. With the Your Algorithm feature, "you can now see the topics we think you're interested in, and change them,...

item.title: Amazon’s Echo speakers can now help kids wind down and fall asleep
item.description: Amazon has launched a new feature for its Echo and Echo Kids smart speakers called Sleep Studio that's designed to make the daily transition to bedtime more enticing for kids and less stressful...

item.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
item.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

item.title: China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors. "The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO...

item.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure. The...

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according...

item.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
item.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

item.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
item.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.
